text:
• You're right to question that! Let me clarify:
Execution control queues exist in MongoD by default - they're a built-in server feature, always active, independent of mongotune. Operations naturally queue there when the server is busy, even with no rate limiting policy running.
Mongotune monitors the execution control queues (passive observation) but controls the ingress rate limiter (active tuning).
Request arrives
[Ingress Rate Limiter) +
[Execution Control queuel +
(Wiredtiger 1
Storage
MONGOTUNE
controls this
monitors this
Mongotune sees dwell time spiking + CPU pressure high
• Mongotune activates the ingress rate limiter to stop more requests from enterin
• Fewer requests → queue drains → dwell time drop
The execution queue dwell time is the symptom mongotune watches. The ingress rate limiter is the lever mongotune pulls.
